key: frazier|2009|critique much of the ptg liter
bare: - **Frazier et al. (2009)** critique: much of the PTG literature relies on retrospective self-report, which has known biases. When growth is measured prospectively (comparing before-trauma and after-trauma measures directly), the effect is much smaller than retrospective studies suggest. Some of what people report as "growth" may be reframing of how they remember their pre-trauma selves, not actual change.

- Frazier, P., et al. (2009). "Does self-reported posttraumatic growth reflect genuine positive change?" *Psychological Science* 20(7): 912–919. — Critical evaluation of retrospective self-report bias in PTG research.
- Frege, G. (1892). "Über Sinn und Bedeutung." *Zeitschrift für Philosophie und philosophische Kritik*, 100, 25-50. [Wikisource (English)](https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/On_Sense_and_Reference)
